description: A signed assertion of a formal property of a lens. Verifications are the formal-channel primitive: they coexist with the emergent channel (encounters, corrections, observations) and neither gates the other.

main.description: A verifier asserts that a lens has a named property, established by a named method, with dependencies and an input space the assertion covers.

lens.description: The lens whose property is being asserted.

kind.description: Fixed taxonomy at the Lexicon level. Each kind has its own trust semantics.

verifier.description: DID of the party asserting the verification.

tool.description: Tool and version used to establish the verification.

inputSpace.description: Description of the input space covered: for property-tests, the generator; for formal-proofs, the domain over which the theorem is stated; for conformance-tests, the standard name and version.

result.description: Assertion outcome. Falsified verifications are first-class records; they are how the community learns a lens is wrong.

counterexample.description: For result=falsified: optional reference to a minimal counterexample.

dependencies.description: Other verifications this one depends on (e.g. a proof assuming a lemma).

proofArtifact.description: For kind=formal-proof: reference to the checkable proof artifact (e.g. Coq/Lean/Agda term). Orchestrators that have the checker may mechanically verify; others take the assertion on trust.
